briefdescription.para: Channel number for Last Aux channel.

sectiondef.header: Indexes for reading the Calibration Coefficient Data.
Indexes for reading the Minimum/Maximum Measurement Data.
Averaging to be done for the channels.
Alarm masks for channels in Configuration registers 1

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor Raw Data to Temperature(centigrades) for On-Chip Sensors.

parameterdescription.para: is the SysMon Raw ADC Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor 12-bit OT Raw Data to Temperature(centigrades) for On-Chip Sensors.

parameterdescription.para: is the SysMon 12-bit Raw ADC Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor Raw Data to Temperature(centigrades) for external reference.

parameterdescription.para: is the SysMon Raw ADC Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor 12-bit OT Raw Data to Temperature(centigrades) for external reference.

parameterdescription.para: is the SysMon 12-bit Raw ADC Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor Raw Data to Voltage(volts) for VpVn supply.

parameterdescription.para: is the System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor Raw Data to Voltage(volts) other than VCCO_PSIO supply.

parameterdescription.para: is the System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts System Monitor Raw Data to Voltage(volts) for VCCO_PSIO supply.

parameterdescription.para: is the System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Temperature in centigrades to System Monitor Raw Data for On-Chip Sensors.

parameterdescription.para: is the Temperature in centigrades to be converted to System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Temperature in centigrades to System Monitor 12-bit OT Raw Data for On-Chip Sensors.

parameterdescription.para: is the Temperature in centigrades to be converted to System Monitor ADC 12-bit Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC 12-bit Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Temperature in centigrades to System Monitor Raw Data for external reference.

parameterdescription.para: is the Temperature in centigrades to be converted to System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Temperature in centigrades to System Monitor 12-bit OT Raw Data for external reference.

parameterdescription.para: is the Temperature in centigrades to be converted to System Monitor ADC 12-bit Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC 12-bit Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Voltage in Volts to System Monitor Raw Data other than VCCO_PSIO supply.

parameterdescription.para: is the Voltage in volts to be converted to System Monitor/ADC Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This macro converts Voltage in Volts to System Monitor Raw Data for VCCO_PSIO supply.

parameterdescription.para: is the Voltage in volts to be converted to System Monitor/ADC Raw Data.

simplesect.para: The System Monitor ADC Raw Data.

briefdescription.para: This data type defines a handler that an application defines to communicate with interrupt system to retrieve state information about an application.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called. It is used to find the device driver instance.

briefdescription.para: This static inline macro calculates the effective baseaddress based on the Sysmon instance.

detaileddescription.para: For PS Sysmon, use additional offset XPS_BA_OFFSET and For PL Sysmon, use additional offset XPL_BA_OFFSET.

parameterdescription.para: is the starting address of the SysMon block in register database.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block or the AMS controller register region.

simplesect.para: Returns the effective baseaddress of the sysmon instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the System Monitor device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
points to the XSysMonPsu device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XSysMonPsu_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XSysMonPsu_CfgInitialize() API.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Upon reset, all Maximum and Minimum status registers will be reset to their default values. Currently running and any averaging will restart. Refer to the device data sheet for the device status and register values after the reset.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the contents of the Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Status Register. Use the XSYSMONPSU_MON_STS_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the ADC conversion in the Single Channel event driven sampling mode.

detaileddescription.para: The EOC bit in Status Register will be set once the conversion is finished. Refer to the device specification for more details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: The default state of the CONVST bit is a logic 0. The conversion is started when the CONVST bit is set to 1 from 0. This bit is self-clearing so that the next conversion can be started by setting this bit.

briefdescription.para: Get the ADC converted data for the specified channel.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the channel number. Use the XSM_CH_* defined in the file xsysmonpsu.h. The valid channels for PS / PL SysMon are 0 - 6, 8 - 10 and 13 - 37. For AMS, 38 - 53 channels are valid.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block or the AMS controller register region.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the ADC converted data for the specified channel. The System Monitor device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of the 16 data read from the device.
Please make sure that the proper channel number is passed.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the calibration coefficient data for the specified parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
specifies the calibration coefficient to be read. Use XSM_CALIB_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu.h to specify the calibration coefficient to be read.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the calibration coefficient. The System Monitor device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of the 16 data read from the device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Minimum/Maximum measurement for one of the XSM_MIN_* or XSM_MAX_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu.h.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
specifies the parameter for which the Minimum/Maximum measurement has to be read. Use XSM_MAX_* and XSM_MIN_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu.h to specify the data to be read.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the maximum/minimum measurement for specified parameter. The System Monitor device guarantees a 10 bit resolution for the ADC converted data and data is the 10 MSB bits of 16 bit data read from the device.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of samples of averaging that is to be done for all the channels in both the single channel mode and sequence mode of operations.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the number of samples of averaging programmed to the Configuration Register 0. Use the XSM_AVG_* definitions defined in xsysmonpsu.h file :
XSM_AVG_0_SAMPLES for no averaging
XSM_AVG_16_SAMPLES for 16 samples of averaging
XSM_AVG_64_SAMPLES for 64 samples of averaging
XSM_AVG_256_SAMPLES for 256 samples of averaging
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the number of samples of averaging configured for all the channels in the Configuration Register 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para:
The averaging read from the Configuration Register 0 is returned. Use the XSM_AVG_* bit definitions defined in xsysmonpsu.h file to interpret the returned value :
XSM_AVG_0_SAMPLES means no averaging
XSM_AVG_16_SAMPLES means 16 samples of averaging
XSM_AVG_64_SAMPLES means 64 samples of averaging
XSM_AVG_256_SAMPLES means 256 samples of averaging

memberdef.argsstring: (XSysMonPsu *InstancePtr, u8 Channel, u32 IncreaseAcqCycles, u32 IsEventMode, u32 IsDifferentialMode, u32 SysmonBlk)
memberdef.name: XSysMonPsu_SetSingleChParams

briefdescription.para: The function sets the given parameters in the Configuration Register 0 in the single channel mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the channel number for conversion. The valid channels are 0 - 6, 8 - 10, 13 - 37.

is a boolean parameter which specifies whether the Acquisition time for the external channels has to be increased to 10 ADCCLK cycles (specify TRUE) or remain at the default 4 ADCCLK cycles (specify FALSE). This parameter is only valid for the external channels.
is a boolean parameter that specifies continuous sampling (specify FALSE) or event driven sampling mode (specify TRUE) for the given channel.
is a boolean parameter which specifies unipolar(specify FALSE) or differential mode (specify TRUE) for the analog inputs. The input mode is only valid for the external channels.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the Configuration Register 0.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled or the input parameters are not valid for the selected channel.
The number of samples for the averaging for all the channels is set by using the function XSysMonPsu_SetAvg.

The calibration of the device is done by doing a ADC conversion on the calibration channel(channel 8). The input parameters IncreaseAcqCycles, IsDifferentialMode and IsEventMode are not valid for this channel.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the alarm outputs for the specified alarms in the Configuration Registers 1:

para.verbatim: - OT for Over Temperature (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_OT_MASK) - ALM0 for On board Temperature (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_TEMP_MASK) - ALM1 for SUPPLY1 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_SUPPLY1_MASK) - ALM2 for SUPPLY2 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_SUPPLY2_MASK) - ALM3 for SUPPLY3 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_SUPPLY3_MASK) - ALM4 for SUPPLY4 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM__SUPPLY4_MASK) - ALM5 for SUPPLY5 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_SUPPLY5_MASK) - ALM6 for SUPPLY6 (XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_SUPPLY6_MASK)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the bit-mask of the alarm outputs to be enabled in the Configuration Registers 1 and 3. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will be disabled. This mask is formed by OR'ing XSYSMONPSU_CFR_REG1_ALRM_*_MASK masks defined in xsysmonpsu.h, but XSM_CFR_ALM_SUPPLY8_MASK to XSM_CFR_ALM_SUPPLY13_MASK are applicable only for PS.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: The implementation of the alarm enables in the Configuration register 1 is such that the alarms for bit positions of 0 will be enabled and alarms for bit positions of 1 will be disabled. The alarm outputs specified by the AlmEnableMask are negated before writing to the Configuration Register 1 because it was Disable register bits. Upper 16 bits of AlmEnableMask are applicable only for PS.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the status of the alarm output enables in the Configuration Register 1.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
SysmonBlk
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.
This is the bit-mask of the enabled alarm outputs in the Configuration Register 1. Use the masks XSYSMONPSU_CFG_REG1_ALRM_*_MASK masks defined in xsysmonpsu.h to interpret the returned value. Bit positions of 1 indicate that the alarm output is enabled. Bit positions of 0 indicate that the alarm output is disabled.

simplesect.para: The implementation of the alarm enables in the Configuration register 1 is such that alarms for the bit positions of 1 will be disabled and alarms for bit positions of 0 will be enabled. The enabled alarm outputs returned by this function is the negated value of the the data read from the Configuration Register 1. Upper 16 bits of return value are valid only if the channel selected is PS.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the specified Channel Sequencer Mode in the Configuration Register 1 :

para.verbatim: - Default safe mode (XSM_SEQ_MODE_SAFE) - One pass through sequence (XSM_SEQ_MODE_ONEPASS) - Continuous channel sequencing (XSM_SEQ_MODE_CONTINPASS) - Single Channel/Sequencer off (XSM_SEQ_MODE_SINGCHAN) - Olympus sampling mode (XSM_SEQ_MODE_OYLMPUS)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the sequencer mode to be set. Use XSM_SEQ_MODE_* bits defined in xsysmonpsu.h.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: Only one of the modes can be enabled at a time.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the channel sequencer mode from the Configuration Register 1.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para:
The channel sequencer mode :
XSM_SEQ_MODE_SAFE : Default safe mode
XSM_SEQ_MODE_ONEPASS : One pass through sequence
XSM_SEQ_MODE_CONTINPASS : Continuous channel sequencing
XSM_SEQ_MODE_SINGCHAN : Single channel/Sequencer off
XSM_SEQ_MODE_OLYMPUS : Olympus sampling mode

briefdescription.para: The function enables the Event mode or Continuous mode in the sequencer mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is a boolean parameter that specifies continuous sampling (specify FALSE) or event driven sampling mode (specify TRUE) for the channel.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

briefdescription.para: The function returns the mode of the sequencer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: Returns the Sequencer mode. XSYSMONPSU_EVENT_MODE for Event mode and XSYSMONPSU_CONTINUOUS_MODE for continuous mode.

briefdescription.para: The function enables the external mux and connects a channel to the mux.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the channel number used to connect to the external Mux. The valid channels are 0 to 5 and 16 to 31.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the Configuration Register 0.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled or the input parameters are not valid for the selected channel.

briefdescription.para: The function returns the external mux channel.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: Returns the channel number used to connect to the external Mux. The valid channels are 0 to 6, 8 to 16, and 31 to 36..

briefdescription.para: The function sets the frequency of the ADCCLK by configuring the DCLK to ADCCLK ratio in the Configuration Register #2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is clock divisor used to derive ADCCLK from DCLK. Valid values of the divisor are PS:
0 means divide by 8.
1,2 means divide by 2.
3 to 255 means divide by that value. PL:
0,1,2 means divide by 2.
3 to 255 means divide by that value. Refer to the device specification for more details.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para:
- The ADCCLK is an internal clock used by the ADC and is synchronized to the DCLK clock. The ADCCLK is equal to DCLK divided by the user selection in the Configuration Register 2.
There is no Assert on the minimum value of the Divisor.

briefdescription.para: The function gets the ADCCLK divisor from the Configuration Register 2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMon instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: The divisor read from the Configuration Register 2.
The ADCCLK is an internal clock used by the ADC and is synchronized to the DCLK clock. The ADCCLK is equal to DCLK divided by the user selection in the Configuration Register 2.

briefdescription.para: The function update the ADCCLK divisor to the Configuration Register 2.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMon instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.
is a pointer to be populated with the divisor code.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if valid divisor found and applied.
XST_FAILURE if valid divisor does not exist.
The ADCCLK is an internal clock used by the ADC and is synchronized to the DCLK clock. The ADCCLK is equal to DCLK divided by the user selection in the Configuration Register 2.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified channels in the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be in the Safe Mode before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels to be enabled. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_CH* defined in xsysmon_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 will be enabled and bit mask of 0 will be disabled. The ChEnableMask is a 64 bit mask that is written to the three 16 bit ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the channels for which the averaging has been enabled in the ADC Channel Averaging Enables Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: The status of averaging (enabled/disabled) for all the channels. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_AVERAGE* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which averaging is enabled and bit mask of 0 are the channels for averaging is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the channel enable bits status from the ADC Channel Selection Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: Gets the channel enable bits. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_CH* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels that are enabled and bit mask of 0 are the channels that are disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the averaging for the specified channels in the ADC Channel Averaging Enable Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be in the Safe Mode before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which averaging is to be enabled. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_AVERAGE* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Averaging will be enabled for bit masks of 1 and disabled for bit mask of 0. The AvgEnableChMask is a 64 bit mask that is written to the three 16 bit ADC Channel Averaging Enable Sequencer Registers.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Averaging Enables Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Analog input mode for the specified channels in the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be in the Safe Mode before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which the input mode is differential mode. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_INPUT_MDE* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to specify the channel numbers. Differential or Bipolar input mode will be set for bit masks of 1 and unipolar input mode for bit masks of 0. The InputModeChMask is a 64 bit mask that is written to the three 16 bit ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Analog input mode for all the channels from the ADC Channel Analog-Input Mode Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: The input mode for all the channels. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_INPUT_MDE* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which input mode is differential/Bipolar and bit mask of 0 are the channels for which input mode is unipolar.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of Acquisition cycles in the ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.

detaileddescription.para: The sequencer must be in the Safe Mode before writing to these registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the bit mask of all the channels for which the number of acquisition cycles is to be extended. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_ACQ* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to specify the Channel numbers. Acquisition cycles will be extended to 10 ADCCLK cycles for bit masks of 1 and will be the default 4 ADCCLK cycles for bit masks of 0. The AcqCyclesChMask is a 64 bit mask that is written to the three 16 bit ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the given values were written successfully to the Channel Sequencer Registers.
XST_FAILURE if the channel sequencer is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the status of acquisition time from the ADC Channel Acquisition Time Sequencer Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: The acquisition time for all the channels. Use XSYSMONPSU_SEQ_ACQ* defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the Channel numbers. Bit masks of 1 are the channels for which acquisition cycles are extended and bit mask of 0 are the channels for which acquisition cycles are not extended.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSysMonPsu *InstancePtr, u8 AlarmThrReg, u16 Value, u32 SysmonBlk)
memberdef.name: XSysMonPsu_SetAlarmThreshold

briefdescription.para: This functions sets the contents of the given Alarm Threshold Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the index of an Alarm Threshold Register to be set. Use XSM_ATR_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu.h to specify the index.
is the 16-bit threshold value to write into the register.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the specified Alarm Threshold Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
is the index of an Alarm Threshold Register to be read. Use XSM_ATR_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu.h to specify the index.
is the value that tells whether it is for PS Sysmon block or PL Sysmon block register region.

simplesect.para: A 16-bit value representing the contents of the selected Alarm Threshold Register.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the conversion to be automatic for PS SysMon.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: In the auto-trigger mode, sample rate is of 1 Million samples.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the AMS monitor status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the monitor status. See XSYSMONPSU_MON_STS_*_MASK definitions present in xsysmonpsu_hw.h for knowing the status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.
points to the XSysMonPsu device configuration structure.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XSysMonPsu_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer, this pointer is then passed as a parameter to the XSysMonPsu_InitInstance() API. Note that this function just initializes the instance and doesn't configure the System Monitor device.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the 64 bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XSYSMONPSU_IER_0_* and XSYSMONPSU_IER_1_* bits defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the 64 bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XSYSMONPSU_IDR_0_* and XSYSMONPSU_IDR_1_* bits defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the enabled interrupts read from the Interrupt Enable Register (IER).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XSYSMONPSU_IER_0_* and XSYSMONPSU_IER_1_* constants defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: A 64-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Mask Registers (IMR1 IMR0).

briefdescription.para: This function returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register(ISR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XSYSMONPSU_ISR_0_* and XSYSMONPSU_ISR_1_ constants defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

simplesect.para: A 64-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Status Registers (ISR1 ISR0).

briefdescription.para: This function clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register (ISR).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

is the 64 bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing the XSYSMONPSU_ISR_0_* and XSYSMONPSU_ISR_1_* bits which are defined in xsysmonpsu_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para:
The test
Resets the device,
Writes a value into the Alarm Threshold register and reads it back for comparison.
Resets the device again.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XSysMonPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the value read from the Alarm Threshold register is the same as the value written.

simplesect.para: This is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer to the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XSysmonPsu_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found.
